topic.title: S1000D 4.x Built-in Support
topic.shortdesc: version 28.1 provides built-in S1000D 4.x support, enabling authors to create, validate, and visually edit S1000D content with BREX-to-Schematron conversion and Smart Paste capabilities.

body.p: S1000D® is the international standard for technical publications in aerospace, defense, and other asset-intensive industries.
Previously, setting up a robust S1000D editing environment in often required custom frameworks, partner solutions, or extensive manual configuration.

version 28.1 changes this with native S1000D 4.x support. You can now author, validate, and visually edit S1000D content out of the box, including BREX editing and conversion, plus migration-friendly Smart Paste.

image.alt: Screenshot showing S1000D data module editing in Oxygen's Author mode

body.p: This article explains the new features and why they matter for anyone who manages a CSDB or writes data modules regularly.

section.title: Out-of-the-Box S1000D
section.p: now includes a dedicated S1000D framework for issues 4.0, 4.1, and 4.2 of the S1000D specification.
When you create a new document from templates, you will find S1000D-specific entries for:

ul.li: Data Modules (DMs)
Publication Modules (PMs)

section.p: Choosing a template provides a correctly structured skeleton document with the appropriate issue-specific namespaces and required elements. This means:

ul.li: No need to copy existing DMs just to get the boilerplate structure.
Consistent, valid starting points across your authoring team.

section.title: Schema-Guided Editing and Validation
section.p: S1000D requires strict adherence to the schema. supports this by providing:

ul.li: Full schema-guided editing for DMs, PMs, and BREX modules
Real-time validation
Context-aware content completion

section.p: As you type, offers only elements and attributes that are valid at the current cursor position, based on the corresponding issue's XSD. This is particularly helpful when working with:

ul.li: The Identification and Status section of a data module
Complex structures in the content section, such as procedures, warnings, and figures
PM constructs that control inclusion and sequencing of DMs

p.b: Built-in Documentation for Elements and Attributes

section.p: When you trigger content completion, displays inline documentation for S1000D elements and attributes:

ul.li: Tooltips in the Content Completion list
Detailed help in the Attributes view

section.p: This provides a quick reference to the specification embedded in your editor—ideal for authors who understand XML but are still learning S1000D semantics.

section.title: Visual Authoring Tailored to S1000D
section.p: You can edit S1000D documents in Author mode using layouts optimized for technical publications:

ul.li: Full-width: Best for day-to-day authoring with maximum screen space.
Print preview: Shows a pagination-oriented layout similar to a delivered publication.
Large-font: Helpful during review sessions or on high-resolution displays.

p.b: Rich-Text Actions Mapped to S1000D Markup

section.p: The Author toolbar provides S1000D-aware formatting controls. Instead of inserting generic <emphasis> tags, inserts the correct S1000D elements and attributes:

ul.li: Bold → <emphasis emphasisType="em01">
Italic → <emphasis emphasisType="em02">
Underline → <emphasis emphasisType="em03">
Overline → <emphasis emphasisType="em04">
Strikethrough → <emphasis emphasisType="em05">
Subscript → <subscript>
Superscript → <superscript>

section.p: Lists and tables are also created and managed using dedicated actions:

ul.li: Insert sequential or random lists (simple or unordered).
Insert definition lists.
Insert and configure tables (header/footer, rows/columns, frame).
Add or delete rows and columns, and join or split cells.

section.p: These actions generate schema-compliant S1000D structures, allowing authors and subject matter experts to work visually without sacrificing validity.
section.note: Use the Outline view alongside Author mode. It reveals the underlying XML structure (DM parts, procedures, figures, etc.), which is invaluable when reconciling CSDB rules with what you see on screen.

section.title: BREX-Aware Workflows
section.p: BREX (Business Rules Exchange) modules are the enforcement layer for your project's specific S1000D implementation. now provides support to:

ul.li: Edit BREX in Text or Author mode with schema-guided completion and validation.
Use the same Author-mode conveniences while defining or updating business rules.

section.p: You can convert BREX modules to Schematron using the "S1000D BREX to Schematron" built-in transformation scenario. This enables several powerful workflows:

ul.li: Integrate BREX-derived rules into your continuous integration or build pipeline.
Run Schematron-based checks as part of 's validation phase, alongside XSD validation.
Maintain a single source of truth: edit business rules in BREX, then regenerate Schematron whenever rules change.

section.note: Store the resulting Schematron in your project and bind it to a custom validation scenario. You can then validate any DM or PM against both the S1000D schemas and your project's BREX rules with a single command.

section.title: Smart Paste for S1000D Documents
section.p: Migrating legacy content (Word, HTML, older manuals) into S1000D is often time-consuming. 's Smart Paste feature significantly reduces that effort:

ol.li: Copy content from a web browser or Word document.
Paste it into an S1000D DM or PM in Author mode.
attempts to preserve structure and formatting, mapping them into valid S1000D constructs (paragraphs, lists, inline emphasis, tables).

section.p: While Smart Paste does not provide fully automated migration, it significantly accelerates the process:

ul.li: Use Smart Paste to bring in the raw narrative and structure.
Then use the S1000D actions and validation to refine the content and enforce project-specific constraints.

section.title: CGM Illustrations in Context
section.p: offers an add-on that provides experimental support for CGM 1.0 images. To enable rendering of CGM images in Author mode, install the CGM support add-on. The add-on:

ul.li: Renders CGM images directly in Author mode, embedded in your S1000D content.
Has been updated to use the latest jcgm 2.0.5 library for improved compatibility and rendering quality.

section.p: To see CGM images inline in your DMs, install the CGM add-on from the Help > Install new add-ons menu.

section.title: Getting Started
section.p: If you are new to S1000D in or are migrating from a custom setup:

ol.li: Open and create a new S1000D document from the templates.
Explore the framework's schema-guided editing in both Text and Author modes.
If you have existing BREX modules, try the BREX-to-Schematron transformation.
Test Smart Paste with sample legacy content to see how it accelerates migration.
Install the CGM add-on if your publications include CGM illustrations.

section.p: With native S1000D 4.x support, removes much of the friction from authoring technical publications in aerospace, defense, and other regulated industries. Whether you are creating new data modules or maintaining a large CSDB, you now have a complete, standards-compliant editing environment ready to use.

section.title: S1000D Support in Web Author
section.p: You can also edit S1000D content directly in , using its built-in support for visual authoring. This support is designed for S1000D issue 4.x data modules and offers an editing environment consistent with the desktop experience.

image.alt: S1000D Data Module edited in Web Author with outline view and visual authoring layout

section.p: The built-in framework includes ready-to-use templates for common S1000D data module types, so authors can start from valid structures instead of building them from scratch. These templates help enforce the S1000D schema rules and reduce the risk of structural errors during authoring.

Smart Paste is also available when editing S1000D content in . When you paste content from external sources (such as Word documents, web pages, or plain text), Smart Paste attempts to convert the pasted content into valid S1000D structures and elements, preserving as much semantic information as possible while maintaining validity.

The visual authoring mode presents S1000D elements using a user-friendly layout and labels, so authors can focus on the content instead of the underlying XML tags. Contextual actions, content completion, and validation feedback are integrated into the editing workflow to help ensure that data modules remain compliant with the S1000D specification.
For more details about the S1000D-related improvements in , see the What's New in Oxygen XML Web Author page.
You can try the S1000D support directly in by opening the online sample available here.

section.title: Conclusion
section.p: 's built-in S1000D 4.x support, available both in the desktop application and in , delivers a comprehensive, production-ready authoring environment for aerospace and defense technical publications. Key benefits include:

ul.li: Zero-configuration setup with ready-made templates and schemas
Schema-guided editing with inline documentation
Visual authoring with S1000D-aware formatting controls
BREX editing and Schematron conversion for business rule enforcement
Smart Paste for accelerated content migration
CGM illustration support for technical graphics
Consistent S1000D authoring experience across desktop and web

section.p: Ready to streamline your S1000D authoring workflow? Download a free trial of Oxygen XML Editor or try Oxygen XML Web Author and experience the difference native S1000D support makes.
For more information:
